ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for stir-fried chives with bean sprouts; mapped to the nearest stir-fried vegetable dish class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Stir-fried vegetables — mixed leafy greens and sprouts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for royale chives (青龙菜) with bean sprouts; A00LC (stir-fried vegetables) used as the closest defensible category anchor.

main_ingredients: royale chives (青龙菜 / garlic chive variety)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of stir-fried royale chives with bean sprouts weighs approximately 250 g, typical of a single-serve vegetable side dish at a Singaporean Chinese hawker stall or casual restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Nutrient Composition of Food: Stir-fried vegetables (garlic chives and bean sprouts, adapted composite)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exists for 'Royale Chives with Bean Sprouts' as a named dish. Values are adapted from HPB Foodo entries for stir-fried garlic chives (kucai goreng) and stir-fried bean sprouts (taugeh goreng), weighted approximately 60:40 by typical dish composition, with soy sauce and oyster sauce seasoning factored in. Energy and macros are consistent with the input record (88 kcal, 2.4 g protein, 6.0 g fat, 4.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g) and are retained as plausible for this preparation. Sodium at 340 mg/100 g reflects soy sauce and oyster sauce contribution.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021 — bean sprouts and leafy green vegetables (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stir-fried royale chives with bean sprouts. GI estimated at 32 based on University of Sydney GI Database values for bean sprouts (GI ~25) and garlic chives / leafy green vegetables (GI ~35–40), weighted by approximate dish composition (60% chives, 40% bean sprouts). Carbohydrate per 100 g is 4.8 g; GL per 250 g serving = (32 × 12.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.8. This is a low-GI, low-GL dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 340 mg per 100 g from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium soy sauce and minimal oyster sauce to bring sodium below 250 mg per 100 g; the potassium content (~220 mg/100 g) from chives and bean sprouts partially offsets sodium's pressor effect, but portion control and sauce reduction remain important.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~32), low carbohydrate (4.8 g/100 g), and low sugar (1.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is well-suited for people with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low glycemic index, low available carbohydrate, and moderate dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g); a standard 250 g plate delivers a glycemic load of approximately 3.8, which has minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 340 mg/100 g and fat 6.0 g/100 g from cooking oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is low in saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g) and contains no trans fat, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, sodium at 340 mg/100 g is borderline elevated and cooking oil quantity should be minimised to reduce total fat intake for individuals managing cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant-rich vegetables with vitamin C (10 mg/100 g) and anti-inflammatory garlic
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Royale chives and bean sprouts provide vitamin C and folate with anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat is low at 1.0 g/100 g with no trans fat, but high-sodium condiments should be replaced with reduced-sodium versions to avoid worsening RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Royale Chives with Bean Sprouts (炒青龙豆芽菜) is a popular Singaporean Chinese vegetable side dish prepared by wok-frying 'Royale Chives' (青龙菜, a variety of garlic chive or similar leafy green) together with crunchy mung bean sprouts in hot oil with garlic, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. The dish is characterised by a tender-crisp texture, a savoury umami flavour from the soy and oyster sauces, and a mild garlicky aroma. It is commonly served as a vegetable accompaniment at hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), and casual Chinese restaurants across Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 88 kcal, 2.4 g protein, 6.0 g fat (predominantly from cooking oil), and 4.8 g carbohydrate with 1.6 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 340 mg/100 g due to soy sauce and oyster sauce. The dish has a low glycemic index (~32) and a glycemic load of approximately 3.8 per standard 250 g plate, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. It is low in saturated fat and contains no trans fat, which is favourable for cardiovascular health. The high vegetable content provides vitamin C (10 mg/100 g), calcium (32 mg/100 g), and iron (0.6 mg/100 g). Sodium reduction through low-sodium condiments is recommended for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or IMR MyFCD entry exists for this specific named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo stir-fried garlic chives and bean sprouts entries and are consistent with the input record. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite adaptation rather than a direct source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this stir-fried composite dish is not available in the HPB Foodo or IMR MyFCD databases as of 2024. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific stir-fried dish preparation are not reported in available approved sources. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Foodo stir-fried vegetable category; no hydrogenated oils used in standard preparation. potassium_mg estimated at 220 mg/100 g based on HPB Foodo values for garlic chives and bean sprouts; this is an estimate and not a directly measured value. Oyster sauce contains shellfish-derived ingredients; dish is not suitable for strict vegetarians or vegans. The dish name 'F2 Royale Chives' is a menu label from GrabFood and is not a canonical food name; canonical English name assigned as 'Stir-Fried Royale Chives with Bean Sprouts'.
